content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660        322-5660           322-5901           322-6441           322-6444

May 26, 1981


James W. McLaughlin
3313 Plateau Drive
Belmont, CA 94002

Dear Mr. McLaughlin:

     This is sent to confirm our telephone conversation in
which we discussed your written request for advice from this
office. You asked whether the fee you receive of $300 per
month for managing the business of the California State
Association of Local Elected Officials (Association) is
reportable income under the Political Reform Act. Your
duties include billing for dues, writing and publishing
bulletins, mailings, correspondence, etc., for the statewide
membership of the Association. The primary goal of the Asso-
ciation is to protect the public's right to elect "those who
hold most important governmental offices."

     This fee would clearly fall within the definition of
"income" in the Political Reform Act, Government Code Section
82030. However, it is my understanding that the disclosure
obligations of the city clerk position in the City of Belmont's
Conflict of Interest Code include the reporting of income
only from business entities. Since the Association is not a
business entity, your income from the Association is not
reportable under the Code.

     Please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901, if
you would like to discuss this letter or if you have further
questions.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Diane Maura Fishburn
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

DMF:plh

Legal Division
F.T.P.C. — 1100 K ST.
Sacramento, Ca 95814 U 12:55PM 01

May 5,1981

9102

Ladies and/or Gentlemen,

As City Clerk of Belmont, I am required to submit
Form 730, Statement of Economic Interest.

In addition to my salary as City Clerk, I receive
a fee of $300 per month for managing the business
of the California State Association of Local Elected
Officials that used to be handled in over office
in Sacramento, including duties such as billing
for dues, writing and publishing bulletins, mailing,
correspondence etc. for the membership which
is statewide and includes most non-legislator
county officials, City Clerks and Treasurers, several
superintendents of schools, a few Councilmen, etc.

Our primary goal is protection of the public
right to elect those who held most important
governmental offices.

I have been advised, by telephone, that this
is not reportable because the association is
not a business and its goal is statewide.

I pay my own dues, receive no reimbursement
of bills type from the City for any attendance at association
meetings (S.C.A.L.O. meetings, made etc.) and I take
vacation days in necessary to any associ-
ation business. I am abusing for any associ-
days on recession. We also have P.O. Box in
Belmont but my checks come from the
Treasurers, Tom Palomares, also in Fairfield.

I would appreciate written confirmation
that I am correct in marking Schedule D as
no reportable interests.

Thank you for your cooperation.

James N. Laughlin
3313 Platean Drive
Belmont, Ca 94002
A City Hall - Belmont

sections.question: Whether the fee of $300 per month received for managing the business of the California State Association of Local Elected Officials (Association) is reportable income under the Political Reform Act, given that the requester is a city clerk required to submit form 730, Statement of Economic Interest.
sections.conclusion: This fee would clearly fall within the definition of "income" in the Political Reform Act, Government Code Section 82030. However, it is my understanding that the disclosure obligations of the city clerk position in the City of Belmont's Conflict of Interest Code include the reporting of income only from business entities. Since the Association is not a business entity, your income from the Association is not reportable under the Code.
sections.question_synthetic: Is the $300 monthly management fee from the California State Association of Local Elected Officials reportable income for a city clerk under the Political Reform Act and local Conflict of Interest Code?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: While the fee qualifies as "income" under the Political Reform Act, it is not reportable under the City of Belmont's Conflict of Interest Code because that code requires reporting of income only from business entities, and the Association is not a business entity.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (dated May 5, 1981) and the FPPC response letter (dated May 26, 1981). Response is from Diane Maura Fishburn, Counsel, Legal Division. Minor spelling variation in address ('Platian' vs 'Plateau') in original documents.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the fee of $300 per month received for managing the business of the California State Association of Local Elected Officials (Association) is reportable income under the Political Reform Act, given that the requester is a city clerk required to submit form 730, Statement of Economic Interest.

CONCLUSION: This fee would clearly fall within the definition of "income" in the Political Reform Act, Government Code Section 82030. However, it is my understanding that the disclosure obligations of the city clerk position in the City of Belmont's Conflict of Interest Code include the reporting of income only from business entities. Since the Association is not a business entity, your income from the Association is not reportable under the Code.
embedding.first_500_words: May 26, 1981 James W. McLaughlin 3313 Plateau Drive Belmont, CA 94002 Dear Mr. McLaughlin: This is sent to confirm our telephone conversation in which we discussed your written request for advice from this office. You asked whether the fee you receive of $300 per month for managing the business of the California State Association of Local Elected Officials (Association) is reportable income under the Political Reform Act. Your duties include billing for dues, writing and publishing bulletins, mailings, correspondence, etc., for the statewide membership of the Association. The primary goal of the Association is to protect the public's right to elect "those who hold most important governmental offices." This fee would clearly fall within the definition of "income" in the Political Reform Act, Government Code Section 82030. However, it is my understanding that the disclosure obligations of the city clerk position in the City of Belmont's Conflict of Interest Code include the reporting of income only from business entities. Since the Association is not a business entity, your income from the Association is not reportable under the Code. Please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901, if you would like to discuss this letter or if you have further questions. Very truly yours, Diane Maura Fishburn Counsel Legal Division May 5, 1981 Legal Division F.P.P.C. - 1100 K St Sacramento, Ca 95814 Ladies and/or Gentlemen, As City Clerk of Belmont, I am required to submit form 730, Statement of Economic Interest. In addition to my salary as City Clerk, I receive a fee of $300 per month for managing the business of the California State Association of Local Elected Officials that used to be handled in our office in Sacramento, including duties such as billing for dues, writing and publishing bulletins, mailing correspondence etc. for the memberships which is statewide and includes most non-legislative county officials, many City Clerks and Treasurers, several Superintendents of Schools, a few Councilmen, etc. Our primary goal is protection of the public right to elect those who hold most important governmental offices. I have been advised, by telephone, that this is not reportable because the association is not a business and its goal is statewide. I pay my own dues, receive no reimbursement of any type from the City for any attendance at association meetings (e.g. mileage, meals etc.) and I take vacation days when necessary for any association business requiring my absence for days on occasion. We have our P.O. Box in Belmont but my checks come from the Treasurer, Tony Kalamaras, architor in Fairfield. I will appreciate written confirmation that I am correct in marking Schedule D as no reportable interests. Thank you for your cooperation. James W. McLaughlin 3313 Platian Drive Belmont, CA 94002 for City Hall - Belmont
embedding.summary: FPPC counsel advises a Belmont city clerk that his $300 monthly fee for managing the California State Association of Local Elected Officials is not reportable income under the city's Conflict of Interest Code, despite meeting the definition of income under the Political Reform Act, because the Association is not a business entity.
